$_description: Distressed, high-upside opportunistic acquisition. Class C asset with significant deferred maintenance and repositioning potential from C to B- through full renovation.

unitMix.$description: Array of unit types with count and rent

renovation.$_description: Detailed renovation plan for repositioning from Class C to Class B-.
renovation.scope: full interior renovation and selective exterior upgrades
commonAreaScope: renovated laundry facility with new machines
renovation.phasingStrategy: Rolling renovation - 10-15 units at a time, maintaining occupancy above 65% during construction

extensionOptions.description: Due diligence extension - 15 days for additional $25K earnest money
Closing extension - 30 days for additional $50K earnest money

keyMetrics.$_description: Pre-calculated key metrics for quick reference.

riskFactors: Environmental contamination risk due to property age (1972) - Phase I and possible Phase II required
Lead-based paint and asbestos likely present - abatement budget included in renovation
Existing code violations must be remediated within 90 days of closing
Flood zone proximity - verify FEMA mapping and insurance requirements
Tenant relocation costs during renovation - budget $2,500/unit temporary relocation
Bridge loan maturity risk - must stabilize and refinance within 3 years
Crime and safety concerns in submarket - budget for security improvements
Construction cost escalation risk - lock contractor pricing pre-closing
notes: Distressed Class C garden-style complex in South Memphis. Property has been family-owned since 1985 with minimal capital investment over the past 15 years. Current owner passed away and estate is liquidating. Significant deferred maintenance including 3 of 5 roofs needing replacement, aged HVAC systems, and outdated electrical in several buildings. 22% vacancy driven by poor condition, not market weakness. Memphis submarket shows strong workforce housing demand with limited new supply. Repositioning strategy: full interior renovation ($25K/unit) over 18 months with rolling unit turnover. Target rent increase of 35-50% post-renovation, repositioning from Class C to solid B-. Bridge financing with renovation holdback; plan to refinance into Agency permanent debt at month 24-30 once stabilized at 90%+ occupancy. Key risk: environmental due to age - budget includes $75K contingency for abatement. Exit strategy: Agency refinance and hold, or sell at 6.5% cap to value-add buyer at Year 5.
